root_text.sn55.40:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn55.40:1.2: Atha kho nandiyo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho nandiyo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.40:1.3: “yasseva nu kho, bhante, ariyasāvakassa cattāri sotāpattiyaṅgāni sabbena sabbaṁ sabbathā sabbaṁ natthi sveva nu kho, bhante, ariyasāvako pamādavihārī”ti.
root_text.sn55.40:2.1: “‘Yassa kho, nandiya, cattāri sotāpattiyaṅgāni sabbena sabbaṁ sabbathā sabbaṁ natthi tamahaṁ bāhiro puthujjanapakkhe ṭhito’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn55.40:2.2: Api ca, nandiya, yathā ariyasāvako pamādavihārī ceva hoti, appamādavihārī ca
root_text.sn55.40:2.3: taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn55.40:2.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho nandiyo sakko bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.sn55.40:3.2: Idha, nandiya, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.40:3.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.40:3.4: So tena buddhe aveccappasādena santuṭṭho na uttari vāyamati divā pavivekāya, rattiṁ paṭisallānāya.
root_text.sn55.40:3.5: Tassa evaṁ pamattassa viharato pāmojjaṁ na hoti.
root_text.sn55.40:4.3: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.40:4.4: So tehi ariyakantehi sīlehi santuṭṭho na uttari vāyamati divā pavivekāya rattiṁ paṭisallānāya.
root_text.sn55.40:4.5: Tassa evaṁ pamattassa viharato pāmojjaṁ na hoti.
root_text.sn55.40:4.12: Evaṁ kho, nandiya, ariyasāvako pamādavihārī hoti.
root_text.sn55.40:5.2: Idha, nandiya, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.40:5.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.40:5.4: So tena buddhe aveccappasādena asantuṭṭho uttari vāyamati divā pavivekāya rattiṁ paṭisallānāya.
root_text.sn55.40:5.5: Tassa evaṁ appamattassa viharato pāmojjaṁ jāyati.
root_text.sn55.40:6.3: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.40:6.4: So tehi ariyakantehi sīlehi asantuṭṭho uttari vāyamati divā pavivekāya rattiṁ paṭisallānāya.
root_text.sn55.40:6.5: Tassa evaṁ appamattassa viharato pāmojjaṁ jāyati.
root_text.sn55.40:6.12: Evaṁ kho, nandiya, ariyasāvako appamādavihārī hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.40:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.40:1.2: Then Nandiya the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.40:1.3: “Sir, if a noble disciple were to totally and utterly lack the four factors of stream-entry, would they live negligently?”
translation_text.sn55.40:2.1: “Nandiya, someone who totally and utterly lacks these four factors of stream-entry is an outsider who belongs with the ordinary persons, I say.
translation_text.sn55.40:2.2: Neverthless, Nandiya, as to how a noble disciple lives negligently and how they live diligently,
translation_text.sn55.40:2.3: listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn55.40:3.1: “And how does a noble disciple live negligently?
translation_text.sn55.40:3.2: Firstly, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.40:3.4: They’re content with that confidence, and don’t make a further effort for solitude by day or retreat by night.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.5: When they live negligently, there’s no joy.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.6: When there’s no joy, there’s no rapture.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.7: When there’s no rapture, there’s no tranquility.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.8: When there’s no tranquility, there’s suffering.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.9: When one is suffering, the mind does not become immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.10: When the mind is not immersed in samādhi, principles do not become clear.
translation_text.sn55.40:3.11: Because principles have not become clear, they’re reckoned to live negligently.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the teaching …
translation_text.sn55.40:4.3: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.4: They’re content with that ethical conduct loved by the noble ones, and don’t make a further effort for solitude by day or retreat by night.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.5: When they live negligently, there’s no joy.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.6: When there’s no joy, there’s no rapture.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.7: When there’s no rapture, there’s no tranquility.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.8: When there’s no tranquility, there’s suffering.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.9: When one is suffering, the mind does not become immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.10: When the mind is not immersed in samādhi, principles do not become clear.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.11: Because principles have not become clear, they’re reckoned to live negligently.
translation_text.sn55.40:4.12: That’s how a noble disciple lives negligently.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.1: And how does a noble disciple live diligently?
translation_text.sn55.40:5.2: Firstly, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.40:5.4: But they’re not content with that confidence, and make a further effort for solitude by day and retreat by night.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.5: When they live diligently, joy springs up.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.7: When the mind is full of rapture, the body becomes tranquil.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.8: When the body is tranquil, they feel bliss.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.9: And when blissful, the mind becomes immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.10: When the mind is immersed in samādhi, principles become clear.
translation_text.sn55.40:5.11: Because principles have become clear, they’re reckoned to live diligently.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the teaching …
translation_text.sn55.40:6.3: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.4: But they’re not content with that ethical conduct loved by the noble ones, and make a further effort for solitude by day and retreat by night.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.5: When they live diligently, joy springs up.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.7: When the mind is full of rapture, the body becomes tranquil.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.8: When the body is tranquil, they feel bliss.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.9: And when blissful, the mind becomes immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.10: When the mind is immersed in samādhi, principles become clear.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.11: Because principles have become clear, they’re reckoned to live diligently.
translation_text.sn55.40:6.12: That’s how a noble disciple lives diligently.”
